summary: Tested iteration 63 features: Top-up ('Recharger') shortcut for current accounts. All backend API tests pass (9/9). Frontend UI tests verified: amber 'Recharger' button visible on all accounts (including fully repaid), modal with live balance calculation, validation for zero/negative amounts, toast notifications, 'Recharges' section in Details panel with amber background.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend current_accounts.py lines 290-327: TopUpBody model and POST /current-accounts/{id}/top-up endpoint correctly validates amount > 0, increments total_advance, and adds top_ups[] entry
Frontend CurrentAccountsTab.jsx lines 156-181: state + helpers openTopUp/saveTopUp correctly manage modal state and API call
Frontend CurrentAccountsTab.jsx lines 345-348: 'Recharger' button (amber) positioned correctly next to 'Enregistrer un remboursement' with data-testid='topup-btn-{acc.id}'
Frontend CurrentAccountsTab.jsx lines 423-441: 'Recharges' section in Details panel with amber background (bg-amber-900/15) and data-testid='top-ups-list-{acc.id}'
Frontend CurrentAccountsTab.jsx lines 719-769: TOP-UP MODAL Dialog with live balance calculation, autoFocus on amount input, default label 'Recharge manuelle'
Frontend validation: saveTopUp() correctly rejects amount <= 0 with toast 'Montant requis (> 0)'
seed_data_creation: Created test accounts (TEST_SmartAlloc_Account_*, TEST_FullyRepaid_Account) for testing. Test data cleaned up after tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 63 added 'Recharger' (top-up) shortcut for current accounts. Key points: (1) New endpoint POST /api/current-accounts/{id}/top-up with body {amount, label?, received_date?}. (2) Amber 'Recharger' button visible on ALL accounts including fully repaid ones (is_fully_repaid=true). (3) Modal shows live balance calculation: 'Le solde actuel passera de X F à Y F'. (4) Validation: amount <= 0 rejected with toast 'Montant requis (> 0)'. (5) Success toast: 'Compte rechargé de XXX F'. (6) 'Recharges' section in Details panel with amber background lists all top_ups[] entries (manual + auto from smart-allocate). (7) Auto top-ups from iter. 61 smart-allocate (label 'Recharge auto pour <description>') also appear in this section.
